meta.title: Jonathan Brownstein — AVP Finance - FP&A

typingPhrases: Corporate FP&A and Financial Analytics Professional
hero.description: Corporate finance and FP&A professional with 15 years of experience across regulated financial services and global industrial manufacturing. Six-year FP&A and controllership foundation inside a multidivisional manufacturer, followed by seven years supporting finance, actuarial, and senior leadership inside the Data Analytics Office of a global reinsurer. Own the forecasting models, budget and variance reporting, and executive financial analysis that leadership uses for planning, performance review, and strategic decisions. Track record includes an automated ARIMA-based forecasting platform that strengthens forecast accuracy before period close, a valuation movement architecture that replaced ad hoc reconciliations with repeatable comparative analysis, and presentation-ready reporting packages for executive audiences. MS in Economics with an Applied Econometrics certificate, BS in Economics, and FLMI from LOMA.

highlights.label: Financial planning and analysis (FP&A) and corporate finance
Financial modeling, trend analysis, and scenario planning

items.title: Senior Data Analyst III
items.context: Senior financial analytics and forecasting lead inside the Data Analytics Office
items.intro: Senior individual contributor and technical lead inside the Data Analytics Office of a global financial services organization. Partner with finance, actuarial, valuation, and risk leadership on forecasting, financial reporting, and executive analysis used in planning cycles and senior management review.

subsections.title: Forecasting, Budgeting, and Financial Modeling
bullets: Designed and deployed an automated ARIMA-based forecasting platform on a scheduled production pipeline. Surfaces anomalies and trend movement before period close, improving forecast accuracy and giving finance leaders forward-looking visibility into portfolio drivers.
Coordinate forecasting inputs and analytical support for in-flight initiatives, translating business assumptions into structured models finance and actuarial partners can stress-test and act on.

Built statistical outlier and anomaly-detection frameworks using probability density function (PDF) methods across large-scale financial data platforms, supporting early identification of shifts that affect reported performance.

subsections.title: Executive Reporting, Benchmark Analysis, and Business Partnership
bullets: Build and maintain Power BI, Tableau, and SSRS reporting that finance leadership and the C-suite use for KPI review, variance analysis, and financial movement commentary. Package complex multi-source results into clear, presentation-ready insight for executive audiences.

Designed the firm's first valuation extract comparison architecture — a repeatable framework for period-over-period movement analysis across reporting segments and source systems that teams now use for comparative performance review and driver analysis.
Partner across finance, actuarial, IT, BI, and compliance in a matrix model to deliver ad-hoc financial analyses, special projects, and governed reporting on deadline.

subsections.title: Process Improvement and Financial Systems
bullets: Set analytics standards on metadata, lineage, data quality, and audit controls across the Data Analytics Office, and architected the Field Inventory Report that became the enterprise reference for governed financial reporting consumption.
Led the classification machine learning rebuild of a regulated reporting workflow, materially improving accuracy, consistency, and audit readiness. The company's former CEO recognized the result.

items.title: Assistant Business Controller
items.organization: Chicago Pneumatic Company LLC (Atlas Copco Group)
items.context: FP&A and corporate finance partner inside a global industrial manufacturer
bullets: Supported annual planning, monthly forecasting, and budgeting for finance, sales, operations, marketing, HR, and regional leadership inside a global multidivisional manufacturer, including P&L and variance analysis and the executive reporting that fed leadership decisions.
Built financial models and automated KPI dashboards for sales, operations, and finance leadership, institutionalizing a consistent reporting cadence and shortening month-end analysis cycles.
Evaluated strategic initiatives and outside vendors on analytics and finance-systems projects, serving as the working bridge between business stakeholders and technical teams on executive priorities.

groups.title: Finance and FP&A: Financial planning and analysis, forecasting and budgeting, financial modeling, P&L and variance analysis, peer and comparative analysis, trend and scenario analysis, strategic initiative support, executive briefing, advanced Excel and VBA
items: Analytics: ARIMA forecasting, statistical outlier detection, applied econometrics
Reporting and Platforms: Power BI, Tableau, SSRS, Python, SQL, R, Databricks, enterprise data warehousing, financial-systems integration
